title: Eagle Club Softball (Fastpitch) Coach
description:
Key Candidate Qualities:

  • Exemplifies the SAS core values of Compassion, Respect, Responsibility, Fairness, and Honesty and enjoys actively contributing towards a positive, professional environment
  • Embrace differences in culture, background, ability, identity, and perspective and believe that they should be respected and celebrated
  • You are the type of person who will enthusiastically step up and help your colleagues when needed or just because it feels like the right thing to do
  • A sense of humor, humility, and optimism, and this is reflected in your work and leadership
  • Be committed to safeguarding children and will willingly commit to and actively follow the safeguarding policies of SAS
  • Familiarity and experience with the Long Term Athlete Development model
  • Performs in a manner that reflects positively on Singapore American School
  • Demonstrates a passion for softball and baseball and a genuine willingness to help our student-athletes reach their maximum potential in the classroom and on the field all while demonstrating SAS Core Values
  • Committed to establishing an environment that fosters positive team attitudes, encourages self-discipline, sportsmanship, leadership, responsibility, and an appreciation for the importance of academics with student-athletes
  • Advise and counsel players who wish to pursue development opportunities and/or collegiate competition, including writing letters of recommendation, if requested
  • Able to communicate clearly with the coaching staff, students, and parents
  • Enjoys working collaboratively

Scope of Responsibilities:

  • Driving force for an Eagle Club Softball development program, forming skills groups and competitive teams for travel tournaments
  • Coordinate and perform administrative duties as assigned by the Head of Baseball/Softball in addition to coaching duties
  • Liaise with on-island teams to coordinate games/events
  • Daily and weekly management and communication of program schedules to families which includes training, scrimmage games, and private lessons
  • Enhancing the program by structuring training drills for members as appropriate, sourcing new coaching techniques and teaching methods
  • Monitoring and reporting individual player progress with an administrative overview
  • Ensuring facilities, equipment, and training methods are in line with SAS standards and protocols
  • Move equipment as required
  • Managing and announcing team rosters for specific tournaments throughout the year
  • Preparing the team for tournament games with drills, exercises, and team talks
  • Coach age group(s) and team(s) assigned by the Head of Baseball/Softball at tournaments
  • Identifying volunteer assistant coaches and team ambassadors who will help coordinate teams as needed
  • Carrying out any administrative matters as needed for the programs assigned by the Head of Baseball/Softball
  • Assisting with player drafting in order to achieve balance among teams
  • Organizing and conducting coaches clinics for volunteer parents
  • Assisting or leading team practices
  • Assisting with Little League in a similar capacity as outlined above, time and resources permitting

Work Schedule

The work schedule shall be set by the Head of Eagle Club Baseball/Softball in conjunction with the SAS School Eagle Activities and Athletics office and will be structured to ensure smooth overall program operations.

​

Qualifications and Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree (or similar), from an accredited college or university, or equivalent professional experience
  • National Federation High School softball coaching certification, or equivalent
  • High School Varsity level playing experience, or equivalent; Pitching/Catching experience preferred
  • Minimum two years of Softball Coaching experience at the youth or High School level
  • American Red Cross First Aid & CPR or equivalent
  • Able to read, write, and perform basic math and should have strong organizational, budgetary, and computer (especially Google Suite, SchoolsBuddy, and Finalsite) skills
  • Excellent written and verbal English communication skills

Required SAS Health, Safety, and Safeguarding Protocols:

  • Ensure that program areas, equipment, and supplies are maintained in an orderly and safe manner.
  • Report any items that may provide a health or safety hazard to staff, members, or guests to your supervisor or the person responsible for those items at your site
  • Follow all policies and procedures related to keeping children safe in our facilities and programs, including those related to managing visitors and guests at our facilities
  • Complete all child safeguarding training as required

postedCompany.description:
SINGAPORE AMERICAN SCHOOL LIMITED

Singapore American School (SAS) is an independent, non-profit, co-educational day school offering an American-based curriculum for preschool through grade 12.  There are more than 3800 student from more than 50 countries and 630 faculty and staff on one campus.
